items.text: How many more chose pizza than tacos?
How many more chose pizza and tacos combined than salad?
How many fewer chose burgers and hot dogs together than pizza?

params.description: Sorting area appears. Three question cards on left. Two drop zones on right labeled JUST COMPARE and COMBINE FIRST, THEN COMPARE.

beats.text: Here's what you've been doing. You look at a problem and decide: Do I just compare? Or do I combine first, then compare? Sort these questions.
Match the task to the group.

validator.description: Student sorted all three questions correctly: q1 to just_compare, q2 and q3 to combine_first

params.description: All three cards lock into their zones to confirm correct sorting.

beats.text: You sorted them all. Words like combined and together tell you to find a total first.

elements.description: Sorting area with three question cards and two drop zones. drag_to_sort tool active.

beats.text: What question do you ask yourself to decide?

options: Does it say combined or together?

condition.selected: Does it say combined or together?

beats.text: That's the key question. The answer tells you whether it's one step or two.
This is strategic thinking. You're not just doing math. You're planning your approach based on what the problem says.
